title: Mobile Companion Workstream
subtitle: Repo-connect, push runtime, EAS, disconnect, and inbox persistence plan for the Living Docs mobile app.
scope: Mobile companion implementation work across tickets #19-#24, with #18 retained as closed historical context.
sourceCoverage: GitHub issues #18-#24 inspected through GitHub tooling on 2026-04-15, plus repo-local mobile source tree.
objective: Keep the mobile companion implementation coherent across UI fidelity, repo connection, push-token runtime behavior, EAS setup, disconnect, and inbox persistence.
successCondition: A Codex worker can pick up the next ticket with dependencies, open decisions, affected files, and verification expectations visible in one document.

callouts.title: How to use this doc
items: Use #19-#24 as the active implementation source of truth. Treat #18 as closed context only.
Before editing `mobile/src/navigation.tsx` or `mobile/src/screens/Repos.tsx`, inspect the existing local worktree changes so unrelated edits are not overwritten.

callout.title: Current pressure pattern
items: #21 now has an implemented code path for runtime detection, Settings messaging, and preview-vs-real repo connect UI; it still needs manual runtime verification.
#24 now has an implemented code path for SQLite persistence, app-level notification capture, read state, swipe delete, and clear-all; it still needs device/runtime verification.
#23 now has an implemented disconnect code path for secret-only and secret-plus-workflow removal; it still needs real GitHub repo verification.
#22 now has `mobile/eas.json` and real-push setup documentation; it still needs a real EAS dev build verification note.

#19 now has a bounded code-alignment pass for empty states, row/header spacing, Repos intro strip, Inbox metadata, native sign-in states, and Doc Detail title alignment; it still needs visual comparison against the mockup canvas.
#20 remains the primary real-GitHub end-to-end gate because connect must be proven before disconnect can be trusted.
The remaining work is now mostly verification: real GitHub, device runtime, EAS build, and pixel comparison.

data.name: Authentication and sign-in surface
notes: The app has authentication code and a sign-in screen, with the native idle, code-issued, and polling states now aligned closer to the mockup spacing, button placement, code-card padding, and polling typography.
The implementation should preserve native device-flow sign-in while keeping the web/PAT path legible as a preview or fallback mode.

data.name: Home screen and document registry
notes: The screen exists and can list available living documents.
The #19 code-alignment pass added the 72x72 empty-state icon tile, exact empty copy, and 14x20 document card spacing.
#18 is useful context for why the mobile app exists, but its scaffolding should not be treated as the active implementation ticket.

data.name: Settings runtime and push status
notes: `mobile/src/notifications.ts` now exposes `pushRuntime()` with the ticket-defined `eas`, `expo-go`, `web`, and `unknown` type contract.
Settings now shows runtime-specific push status copy for EAS builds, Expo Go, and web preview mode instead of silently degrading when no push token exists.
The #19 code-alignment pass tightened section-label spacing, row padding, info-label sizing, and subtitle line-height toward the mockup.

data.name: Connect-a-repo flow
notes: The code surface for repo connection exists, but #20 still needs a real throwaway-repo end-to-end verification pass.
When no real push token is available, Repos now writes the explicit placeholder token and shows warning-toned preview copy instead of claiming full push delivery.
When a real token is available, Repos writes the real token and uses the success path; connected rows can be reconnected to overwrite older placeholder secrets.
The #19 code-alignment pass removed the extra in-screen title so the stack header owns the page title and tightened row typography/button dimensions.

data.name: Disconnect repo flow
notes: `mobile/src/github-api.ts` now exposes `deleteRepoSecret`, `deleteWorkflowFile`, and `disconnectRepo` with missing-secret and missing-workflow cases treated idempotently.
Connected and preview-connected rows now expose an ellipsis action that can remove only the secret or remove both the secret and workflow file.
The row refreshes connection status after a successful disconnect, so secret-only removal should show `Partial` when the workflow remains.

data.name: Persistent inbox and notification audit trail
notes: Notifications now persist through `expo-sqlite` in `mobile/src/inbox-store.ts` instead of a module-scoped array.
Notification capture has moved to `mobile/App.tsx`, while the Inbox screen subscribes to the store and renders the event log.
Rows support read state, swipe-to-delete, and a clear-all action with confirmation.
The #19 code-alignment pass added the empty notification icon tile, 14x20 row spacing, and relative-time/source metadata.

data.name: Web runtime
notes: `pushRuntime()` now returns `web` when `Platform.OS === 'web'`.
Settings and Repos label web as preview-only and route repo setup through the placeholder-token path.

data.name: Expo Go runtime
notes: `pushRuntime()` now returns `expo-go` when `Constants.appOwnership === 'expo'`.
Settings and Repos label Expo Go as preview-only and point the user toward an EAS dev build for real pushes.

data.name: EAS/dev-build runtime
notes: `mobile/eas.json` now defines development, preview, and production profiles.
`mobile/README.md` now documents Expo login, `npx eas-cli init`, iOS and Android development builds, install flow, first-launch permission, and Settings verification.
`app.json` intentionally does not commit `extra.eas.projectId`; `eas init` writes account-specific project identity.

data.name: GitHub repo artifact status
notes: Repo status distinguishes missing workflow, missing secret, partial setup, and ready setup where possible.
GitHub secrets are write-only, so secret presence and secret value cannot be treated as a reliable readable source of truth after creation.
Disconnect now reuses the same status refresh path after removing the secret and optionally deleting `.github/workflows/living-doc-notify.yml`.

data.name: Local mobile state stores
notes: #21 may need local connection metadata to preserve preview-vs-real state across refreshes.
#24 now introduces `mobile/src/inbox-store.ts` as an application-level SQLite event log rather than screen-local state.

data.name: GitHub API client
data.purpose: Create, inspect, and eventually remove GitHub repository artifacts for the mobile companion connection.
data.whenToUse: Use when implementing or verifying connect, status refresh, or disconnect behavior.
data.invocation: Called by the mobile app screens, not directly from the CLI.
notes: The API surface should be verified against a real throwaway repository before being treated as trusted.
The client now includes the disconnect side of the lifecycle: delete secret, delete workflow file, and refresh status.
caveats: GitHub secret values are not readable after write, so status logic needs a separate source for preview or real-token state.

data.name: GitHub secret sealed-box encryption
data.purpose: Encrypt the push token for GitHub Actions secrets using GitHub's public key flow.
data.whenToUse: Use during connect-repo verification and any debugging of 422 or malformed-secret errors.
data.invocation: Called from `mobile/src/github-api.ts` when writing the repository secret.
caveats: If GitHub rejects secret creation, first validate sealed-box output, UTF-8/base64 handling, key IDs, and public-key fetch behavior.

data.name: Living-doc notification workflow template
data.purpose: Install the GitHub Actions workflow that notifies the mobile companion when a living document changes.
data.whenToUse: Use in connect, status refresh, and disconnect work where workflow contents or path behavior matter.
data.invocation: Written through GitHub Contents API by the mobile app.
caveats: Disconnect semantics need to define whether the workflow is removed, disabled, or left in place without a secret.

data.name: Mobile mockup canvas
data.purpose: Provide the visual reference for pixel-matching the mobile screens.
data.whenToUse: Use before editing any mobile screen layout or theme work covered by #19.
data.invocation: Open `docs/mobile-mockups.html` and compare against the running app.
caveats: The current tickets do not define an automated screenshot diff harness, so verification is still manual unless one is added.

data.name: EAS dev-build setup
data.purpose: Make real native push-token testing reproducible outside Expo Go.
data.whenToUse: Use after #21 labels runtime modes and before claiming push notifications are production-real.
data.invocation: `cd mobile && npx eas-cli build --profile development --platform ios` or `npx eas-cli build --profile development --platform android`.
caveats: Requires an Expo account and `npx eas-cli init` before a contributor can build.
No successful device build has been recorded in `mobile/README.md` yet.

data.name: SQLite inbox store
data.purpose: Persist notification events and user-read state for the mobile Inbox.
data.whenToUse: Use when implementing the persistent Inbox and when deciding where notification listeners write events.
data.invocation: Imported by application-level notification handling and `mobile/src/screens/Inbox.tsx`.
caveats: Typecheck passes, but restart persistence, swipe gestures, and notification response navigation still need manual device/runtime verification.

data.name: Pixel-match mobile screens
data.currentCoverage: A bounded #19 code pass aligned Home and Inbox empty icon tiles, Home/Inbox row and header spacing, Repos intro and row/button typography, Settings label/row details, Inbox relative-time/source metadata, native Sign In state spacing, and centered Doc Detail title behavior. `npm run typecheck` passes.
data.nextStep: Run the mobile app at an equivalent 393x852 viewport and compare Sign In, Home, Doc Detail, Settings, Repos, and Inbox against `docs/mobile-mockups.html`.
notes: This should remain a verification gate because visual parity cannot be proven from code inspection alone.
gaps: No explicit device/simulator capture workflow is documented in the ticket.
No pixel-diff threshold or artifact storage path is defined.

data.name: Connect-a-repo end-to-end
data.currentCoverage: Code exists for API client, encryption, and workflow content, but the issue explicitly asks for real throwaway-repo verification.
data.nextStep: Use a real test repository to verify secret creation, workflow write/update behavior, status refresh, and workflow execution.
gaps: No verified note has been added to `mobile/README.md` yet.
If testing from web or Expo Go, #21 may force placeholder behavior instead of a real push token.

data.name: Push-token runtime matrix
data.currentCoverage: `pushRuntime()` exists, Settings consumes it, and Repos now differentiates real and preview connection paths with distinct copy, toast tone, and row pills. `npm run typecheck` passes.
data.nextStep: Manually verify the matrix in web, Expo Go, and an EAS/dev build; then update #21 with runtime screenshots or notes.
gaps: GitHub secrets cannot prove after the fact whether a stored value was a real Expo token or a placeholder.
This implementation keeps preview state explicit in the current runtime and current session; a durable readable preview marker is still deferred unless #20 or #23 requires it.

data.name: EAS dev-build verification
data.currentCoverage: `mobile/eas.json` exists, `mobile/README.md` documents the real-push build flow, and `npm run typecheck` passes.
data.nextStep: Run `npx eas-cli init`, produce an iOS or Android development build, verify Settings shows `Ready...`, and add a `Verified EAS build on YYYY-MM-DD` note to `mobile/README.md`.
gaps: No real EAS development build has been recorded yet.
A real device build requires Expo credentials outside the repo.

data.name: Disconnect repo verification
data.currentCoverage: `disconnectRepo()` exists, connected rows open a destructive action sheet, secret-only removal and secret-plus-workflow removal are implemented, and `npm run typecheck` passes.
data.nextStep: Verify both disconnect modes against a throwaway GitHub repo after the connect end-to-end path in #20 is proven.
gaps: No real GitHub repository verification has been recorded yet.
Manual workflow-run behavior after secret-only disconnect still needs to be checked for the expected missing-secret failure.

data.name: Persistent inbox verification
data.currentCoverage: `expo-sqlite` is installed, `mobile/src/inbox-store.ts` exists, App-level notification ingestion writes the store, and Inbox supports list, mark-read, swipe delete, and clear-all. `npm run typecheck` passes.
data.nextStep: Manually verify notification receipt, restart persistence, tap-to-read navigation, swipe delete, and clear-all on device or simulator.
gaps: No runtime/device verification has been recorded yet.
The ticket still does not define retention or pruning for old events.

data.name: #18 is closed context, not active implementation scope
notes: #18 remains useful for intent and history, but #19-#24 are the actual work breakdown.

data.name: Choose how preview connection state survives refresh
notes: Because GitHub secrets are write-only, the app cannot infer real-vs-placeholder token state from the secret value after creation.
The #21 implementation uses explicit current-runtime and current-session preview labeling without introducing a readable repo marker.
If #20 or #23 needs durable preview-vs-real recovery after refresh or reinstall, promote this into a concrete local metadata or repo-marker ticket.

data.name: Own notification capture above the Inbox screen
notes: Notification capture now lives in `mobile/App.tsx` so events can be persisted even when the Inbox screen is not mounted.
The Inbox screen renders and mutates persisted events through `mobile/src/inbox-store.ts`; it no longer owns the push-capture side effect.

data.name: Decide EAS project ownership before committing canonical config
notes: `app.json` should not commit a random contributor's `extra.eas.projectId`.
Triadflow-owned builds should use the shared Expo project; fork or contributor testing can use a local Expo project and keep that project ID out of shared commits.

data.name: A single GitHub secret models one active mobile endpoint
notes: The current secret-based connection model appears to support one notification target per connected repository unless the workflow and token model are expanded.
Multi-device or team notification delivery should remain out of scope unless a new ticket defines it.
